mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of rising consumer credit balances and contracting industrial production indicates demand-pull inflation outpacing supply, which forces restrictive policy and depresses equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both proxies move in opposite directions, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of housing market contraction, a decline in new housing starts amplifies negative sentiment about economic growth, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month, indicating housing data is decoupled from equity pricing in this period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, which directly supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show positive growth while SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 7.0% year-over-year growth, it signals entrenched inflation that erodes real yields and forces aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when CPI crosses the 7.0% threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and robust consumer credit expansion indicate sustained aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both employment and credit data show synchronized strength, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment, a sustained increase in industrial production reflects improved capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which boosts profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average, indicating supply improvements are already fully priced in or offset by demand weakness.
